title: Meta Now Lets Anyone Use Your Instagram Photos in AI Images—Unless You Opt Out
gist: Meta launched its first AI image model, Muse Image, from Meta Superintelligence Labs to rival OpenAI's GPT Images 2.0 and Google's Nano Banana 2, and quietly opted public Instagram profiles into having their photos used in others' AI generations by default, notification-free, unless users dig into settings to opt out.

insights.claim: Meta launched its inaugural AI image model, Muse Image, from Meta Superintelligence Labs, positioning it against OpenAI's GPT Images 2.0 and Google's Nano Banana 2 in the AI image generation race.
insights.speaker: Reece Rogers
insights.so_what: Confirms Meta is now competing directly in generative image models against OpenAI and Google.
insights.quote: Meta launched its inaugural AI image model from the Meta Superintelligence Labs on Tuesday
insights.locator: Meta launched its inaugural AI image model

insights.claim: Muse Image rolled out with deep integration into Instagram, letting anyone tag a public profile in a prompt and generate an image using that person's likeness via Meta AI.
insights.speaker: Reece Rogers
insights.so_what: Meta is leveraging its social graph as a distribution and differentiation moat for AI image tools.
insights.locator: The new model, called Muse Image

insights.claim: Public Instagram profiles are automatically opted into being used as fodder for others' AI image generations, and users must switch to private or toggle off a buried 'Sharing and reuse' setting to prevent it.
insights.speaker: Reece Rogers
insights.so_what: Reinforces the platform pattern of opt-out defaults that harvest user content for AI, a recurring regulatory/privacy flashpoint.
insights.quote: public Instagram profiles are now automatically opted into being fodder for generative AI remixes.
insights.locator: The new model, called Muse Image

insights.claim: Users are not notified when someone generates AI images from their Instagram content, and already-created AI images are not deleted even after a user switches to private or changes settings.
insights.speaker: Reece Rogers
insights.so_what: The lack of notification and non-deletion raises consent and liability exposure for Meta.
insights.quote: You will not be notified about content created using AI features at Meta
insights.locator: It's also notable that Instagram users may be remixing

insights.claim: The author frames Meta's move as part of a broader industry pattern of opt-out rather than opt-in AI defaults, likening it to Google Search storing reverse-image-search media uploads to train its AI.
insights.speaker: Reece Rogers
insights.so_what: Signals rising consumer/regulatory friction over default AI-training consent across major platforms.
insights.quote: just like how Google Search now stores media uploads, like from reverse image searches, to train its AI
insights.locator: Even though it's overwhelming just how often companies
